mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period, or if the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a certain threshold, it signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% over a 10-year period, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust industrial production indicates broad economic strength, suggesting healthy consumer and business demand, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous strong growth in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 returns more than 40% of the time over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate during an economic expansion signals full employment, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently underperforms (e.g., average return below 0.1% over 5 days) during periods when UNRATE is below 6.0% in an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and can trigger tighter monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns as future earnings are devalued.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 returns are consistently positive during periods of high and accelerating CPI, or if the relationship is statistically insignificant over a 15-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects underlying economic confidence and investment, which contributes to broader economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns as construction and related industries thrive.
failure_condition: If HOUST growth consistently leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period, or if the correlation between HOUST growth and SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a falling federal funds rate indicates a recessionary environment where the central bank is easing policy to stimulate the economy, but the immediate impact on equities is often negative due to underlying economic weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS falls > 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE rises significantly while FEDFUNDS falls, over a 15-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant decline in GDP signals weakening corporate earnings and consumer demand, leading to sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence erodes.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive during more than 50% of periods when GDPC1 shows negative growth in a contractionary regime, or if the average return is not significantly negative.
